w:r.w:t: Full-Stack | TypeScript + Python | ThinkRoot × Vortex Hackathon 2026
Resilience-First · Dead Reckoning · ML-Powered ETA · Safety-First Design

IZUM is a resilience-first campus bus tracking platform built on a TypeScript + Python full-stack. The architecture is designed around three core principles: GPS dead reckoning for continuous position interpolation, an ML-powered ETA model using 8 signal-aware features, and an honest status system that communicates network degradation without alarming users.
The system comprises five distinct layers communicating via WebSocket streams, REST APIs, Redis pub/sub, and PostgreSQL persistence.
Emits position deltas (dlat, dlng, dhdg, speed) with configurable network tiers
Auth, WebSocket relay, REST endpoints, background jobs, ML proxy
8-feature RandomForestRegressor, dead reckoning projection, uncertainty calc
Real-time bus state (TTL), pub/sub broadcast, persistent user/fleet/safety data
Student, Driver, Admin apps + Static Docs site
The following describes the complete path from GPS hardware to a student seeing a live bus marker:
Emits dlat, dlng, dhdg, speed, tier every 5–30s (configurable)
Receives ping, validates JWT, writes bus:{id}:state/position/meta to Redis with TTL
ioredis SET EX — 120s TTL drives state machine
PUBLISH — all WS subscriber instances receive it
Fans out message to all connected frontend WebSocket clients for that bus
broadcast() — fire and forget, no ACK wait
Receives message, updates Zustand store — vehicleState, lat, lng, tier, confidenceRadius
Every 100ms: projects new lat/lng from last speed + heading; calls setLatLng on Leaflet marker
Computes divergence from interpolated pos; lerp corrects if <5m, snap if ≥5m
haversine distance check → smooth or snap
GET /api/eta/{stop_id} → Fastify proxies to Python ML; assembles 8-feature vector, runs RF inference
Reads vehicleState from shared Zustand — renders appropriate bar text, color, animation
Zero additional API calls — pure state machine
All real-time position updates flow through a single compact message format, reducing payload by 94% vs naive JSON:
ACTIVE | SPARSE | LOST | BUFFERING | SYNCING — drives UI state machine
Current bus coordinates — updated by dead reckoning between pings
Degrees 0–360 — used by dead reckoning to project next position
Vehicle speed — multiplied by elapsed time in reckoning loop
FULL | REDUCED | MINIMAL | DEAD — controls ETA confidence width
Circle radius in metres shown on map — grows as signal weakens
Round-trip time — displayed in Network Toggle panel live stats
GPS events queued during DEAD mode — shown in status bar text
Server timestamp — used to compute ping_gap_seconds for ML features

The Fastify server is the central API gateway. It handles all authentication, routes REST requests, manages WebSocket connections, proxies ML requests to Python, and runs background jobs for ghost bus detection and late night monitoring.
POST /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/refresh, /auth/logout, GET /auth/me — RS256 JWT with httpOnly refresh cookie
WS /ws/bus/{bus_id} — authenticate, subscribe Redis channel:bus:{id}, broadcast to all clients
Forwards /api/eta/* to Python FastAPI microservice; caches responses in Redis for 15s
Every 60s: scans Redis bus state for ghost bus conditions; every 60s: checks late night driver check-in
POST /api/simulator/network-mode → Python simulator; adjusts RTT, payload, interval live

A separate FastAPI service loaded with a pre-trained RandomForestRegressor model. Inference is < 5ms. The service reads bus state from Redis, assembles the 8-feature vector, and returns a confidence-aware ETA range.
Primary predictor — bus position in sequence vs destination stop
Fast bus → lower ETA; stationary bus → ghost bus suspect
Rush hour (8–9am) vs off-peak changes ETA significantly
Weekend schedules differ; Monday congestion patterns
Directly drives confidence_width — tier 3 (DEAD) widens range 2.5×
Staleness of position data — grows linearly in uncertainty formula
Buffered events indicate how much position recovery is pending
Network quality proxy — high RTT correlates with unreliable future pings
state (ACTIVE/SPARSE/LOST…), last updated — expires → drives LOST transition
lat, lng, heading, speed, ts — read by ML + WS snapshot
GPS events queued during DEAD mode — score = ts; flushed on reconnect
Broadcast channel — Fastify publishes, all WS handlers subscribe
Access token JTI → user_id; DEL on logout for instant revocation
Refresh token JTI → user_id (7 days); DEL on logout
Cached ML ETA response — prevents hammering Python service every 30s poll
last_checkin_ts, overdue (bool), deviation_detected — fast UI reads
id (UUID), email, hashed_password, role, verified, created_at
Single source of identity — students, drivers, admins all share this table
Student profile — college_id and phone hashed for privacy
id, user_id (FK), license_no, bus_id (FK nullable), active
Driver profile — bus_id null until admin assigns route
Fleet registry — each bus has one route
Route geometry — ordered lat/lng waypoints used for deviation detection
id, route_id (FK), name, lat, lng, sequence
Ordered stop list — sequence used for stops_remaining ML feature
JWT refresh token store — supports rotation and instant revocation
id, student_id_hash, bus_id, lat, lng, ts, status, cancelled
Safety log — student_id hashed; never stored in plain form
token, student_id, bus_id, destination_stop_id, expires_at, used
Shareable journey links — 2hr expiry enforced at DB level
Audit trail — every check-in logged; used for missed-check-in alert
id, bus_id, type, stationary_minutes, detected_at, resolved_at
Ghost bus + deviation events — persists after Redis TTL expires
Student-submitted ghost bus reports — reviewed by admin
VIEW — avg arrival by stop_id + hour + day_of_week
Fallback ETA when model operates on dead reckoning data

The state machine is the heart of IZUM's resilience design. Redis TTLs drive automatic state transitions — if no ping arrives before a key expires, state degrades automatically without any explicit timeout logic in application code.
When no WS ping arrives, the frontend projects position every 100ms using the last known speed and heading. This ensures the marker always moves — even on degraded signal.
dist = (speed_kmh / 3.6) × 0.1 → converts km/h to m/s, multiplies by 100ms
haversine(interpolated, real_ping) — if ≤ 5m: lerp correction over 500ms; if > 5m: snap + expand circle
timestamp diff check — actual position update only if > 100ms since last; rAF runs at 60fps
setLatLng() skipped if position delta < 0.5m — prevents redundant DOM updates at near-zero speed
Live map (P1), Status bar (P4) — persistent connection, JWT auth in Upgrade header
Registration (R1) — bcrypt hash, Prisma transaction, RS256 JWT pair response
Registration (R1) — real-time availability check, debounced 400ms, rate limited
Login (R2) — httpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie for refresh token
All protected pages — silent renewal; cookie sent automatically
Login (R2) — DEL Redis session:{jti} + refresh:{jti}; clears cookie
Login (R2), Live map (P1), Stop-Centric (P2) — session restore on page load
Live map (P1), Network toggle (P5), Ghost bus (P6), SOS (P7)
Live map WS fallback (P1), Virtual escort (P8) — 30s HTTP poll backup
Status bar (P4), Ghost bus (P6) — 60s background poll; returns flagged buses
Ghost bus (P6) — student submits report; INSERT anomaly_reports
Late night (P9) — every 60s; returns last_checkin_ago_s, next_due_in_s, overdue
Late night (P9) — driver-only JWT; resets 10-min countdown ring
Late night (P9) — compares actual position vs route waypoints
Live map (P1), Stop-centric (P2), Virtual escort (P8) — cached in Zustand
Live map (P1), Stop-centric (P2) — 30s poll; Redis cached 15s
ETA detail (P3) — 20s poll; full model_features + uncertainty_bar_pct
Network toggle (P5) — admin/demo only; adjusts RTT, payload, interval live
Network toggle (P5) — page load; returns current mode config
Network toggle (P5) — resets route + mode to Full Signal defaults
Silent SOS (P7) — fires after 10s cancel window; student_id from JWT not body
Virtual escort (P8) — generates 16-byte hex token; 2hr expiry in DB
Virtual escort parent view (P8) — token auth only; relays Redis position
Admin dashboard — fleet overview with all bus states
Landing page (P10) — checks live demo reachability; show/hide CTA button

Three rapid taps on a near-invisible shield icon (opacity 20%) within 800ms triggers a 10-second cancel window, then silently dispatches to campus security. No visible alarm. No red text. Haptic feedback only on mobile.
Invisible to bystanders — student can trigger without being seen
Prevents accidental triggers from normal map interaction
Breathing room for accidental triggers — cancel bar drains visibly at top of screen
Bystander-proof — no visual tells. Only haptic vibration on the student's phone
Student ID from JWT, not body
Prevents spoofed SOS — server extracts student_id from token sub claim
If geolocation API is denied, uses Redis bus:{id}:position as location
SHA-256 hash — SOS events are pseudonymous; not linked to personal records by default
Prevents spam while preserving genuine emergency capacity
Campus security alert sent before SOS row is even committed to DB

A student shares a token-based URL (expires in 2 hours) with a parent or warden. The escort viewer polls every 15 seconds — no login required, no app install. Shows bus position, route progress, and arrival status.

Activates automatically after 21:00. Overlays a deep dark theme on the map and adds a 10-minute driver check-in countdown ring. A missed check-in fires an immediate security alert via Resend. Route deviation beyond 100m highlights expected vs actual path.
Ring stops, shakes, security email dispatched
React Leaflet · rAF dead reckoning · WS
SVG inline · D3 · Floating UI tooltips
The project is structured into 6 sequential phases. Each phase produces independently testable, deployable components. Later phases build on earlier foundations without requiring rewrites.
Duration: Days 1–3 | Milestone: Live database + Auth API working
npm scripts: dev, build, lint all green; tsconfig strict mode
prisma migrate dev creates all tables; seed script populates test fleet
PING returns PONG; SET/GET/EXPIRE working in integration test
Private key signs, public key verifies; keys stored in /secrets (gitignored)
Returns 201 with JWT pair; 409 on duplicate email; bcrypt cost 12
httpOnly cookie strategy; Redis session store; instant revocation on logout
Returns user object from JWT; used by all protected pages for session restore
Rejects requests with expired/invalid tokens with 401 before route handler runs
Registration, login, email-check limits configured; returns 429 on breach
Initialise React + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind frontend
npm run dev starts; HMR working; Tailwind JIT active
React Hook Form + Zod validation; POST /auth/register; role selector; redirect on success
httpOnly cookie flow; Axios interceptor for 401 → auto-refresh; role-based redirect
Stores access_token + user in memory; cleared on logout; persists across tab focus
Duration: Days 4–6 | Milestone: Live bus marker moving on a map in browser
Emits dlat, dlng, dhdg, speed, tier every 5s; configurable route loop
POST /simulator/network-mode switches RTT target, payload size, interval live
Upgrades connection, verifies JWT in Upgrade header, subscribes to Redis channel
Each ping writes 3 hash keys with TTL; correct TTL drives state transitions
Publish on channel:bus:{id}; all WS handlers receive and fan out
State degrades ACTIVE→SPARSE→LOST→BUFFERING automatically on TTL miss
OpenStreetMap tiles loading; MapContainer centred on campus
Connects to /ws/bus/{id}; updates vehicleState, lat, lng, tier, confidence_radius_m
Marker moves every 100ms from speed + heading; haversine divergence check
Radius = confidence_radius_m from WS message; hidden in ACTIVE state
GET /api/stops once on load; rendered as Leaflet markers with name popup
Axios polls GET /api/bus/{id}/position every 30s when WS connection drops
Phase 3 — ML ETA Service & Student Views
Duration: Days 7–9 | Milestone: ETA cards live with confidence ranges
Synthetic dataset: 8 features × route; model.score() ≥ 0.85; saved as eta_model.joblib
GET /eta/{stop_id}: assembles feature vector from Redis, runs inference, returns eta_min/max/confidence
confidence_width = base × signal_factor × staleness_factor; uncertainty_bar_pct clamped 0–100
Fastify ML proxy + Redis ETA cache
Proxies to Python; caches eta:{stop_id} for 15s; reduces ML calls by ~90% at peak
Stop cards with ETABadge, LeaveNowPill, ConfidenceRing; Fuse.js search; 30s poll
calcLeaveNow() compares eta_min to walkTime preference; LEAVE_NOW / LEAVE_SOON / WAIT
Full model_features panel; UncertaintyBar width from uncertainty_bar_pct; 20s poll
FallbackBanner slides down when dead_reckoning = true; shows historical_avg_arrival
ETA Detail state machine (4 display modes)
FULL/REDUCED/MINIMAL/LOST map to bar width, fill color, text format, banner visibility
Reads vehicleState from shared WS Zustand; AnimatePresence mount/unmount; 6 states mapped
GET /api/bus/anomalies every 60s; triggers GHOST_BUS bar state when non-empty
Duration: Days 10–11 | Milestone: SOS, Escort, Late Night all functional
Extracts student_id from JWT sub; hashes before INSERT sos_events; triggers Resend within 2s
3-tap detection in 800ms; 10s cancel bar drain; vibration API; toast slide-up; screen flash
crypto.randomBytes(16) token; INSERT escort_tokens with expires_at; returns shareable URL
Validates token + expiry; reads Redis position; returns lat/lng, eta, journey_complete
ShareJourneyButton → modal with URL; navigator.share() on mobile
Token from URL path; 15s poll; read-only Leaflet map; RouteProgressBar; JourneyCompleteScreen
GET /checkin-status returns overdue flag; POST /checkin driver-only JWT; setInterval alert scanner
GET /api/bus/{id}/deviation: computes nearest route waypoint, returns deviation_meters
21:00 auto-activation; SVG countdown ring; overdue shake; deviation highlight on Leaflet
Node setInterval 60s: scans bus state Redis keys; flags stationary > 15min; INSERTs anomaly_log
60s poll; AnimatePresence bounce-in; SVG countdown ring; Report button POSTs anomaly_report
Configured for SOS, missed check-in, route deviation — all fire within 2s of detection
Phase 5 — Admin, Demo Controls & Docs Site
Duration: Days 12–13 | Milestone: Full judge-facing demo experience ready
4 mode cards with glow + pulse; POST on tap; LiveStatsBar with 2s counter animation
React Router redirect to /map if role !== admin|demo; server-side role check on POST
Returns all buses with current state, driver, route — admin dashboard data
tsParticles hero; Framer scroll triggers; Leaflet demo loop; /health reachability check for CTA
Inline SVG system diagram with CSS dot animation; D3 state machine; Floating UI tooltips
Shiki static syntax highlight; EndpointAccordion AnimatePresence; copy-to-clipboard on all blocks
Vite dist/ deployed; all 3 docs pages (P10–P12) load < 1.5s LCP; no server required
JS bundle < 120KB gzip; Tailwind purge; Leaflet lazy-loaded; tsParticles lite variant
Duration: Day 14 | Milestone: Production-ready, judge demo scripted
Registration → Login → WS connect → ETA → SOS flow all passing
Security audit: JWT, cookies, rate limits
No localStorage token storage; httpOnly cookie verified; rate limits fire correctly
student_id, phone, college_id all hashed before DB insert; no plaintext PII in logs
50 concurrent WS clients receive broadcast within 100ms; no head-of-line blocking
TTL expiry correctly transitions state ACTIVE→SPARSE→LOST→BUFFERING; SYNCING on reconnect
Judge can toggle Full→Reduced→Minimal→Dead and see status bar + ETA range change in real time
3-tap on shield; cancel window; confirmation toast — judge can perform in < 30 seconds
Override clock to 21:00; countdown ring visible; simulate missed check-in → alert fires
Simulator configured as stationary; anomaly detected within 60s; modal appears
Fastify + Python services deployed; env vars set; health checks passing
All 12 pages consistent with deployed API; no dead links; payload comparison table accurate
Type-safe forms, API calls, WS handling
Build tool — fast dev server + tree-shaken production bundle
Page transitions, status bar animations, SOS flash
Client state — auth tokens, vehicleState, ETA data
Uncontrolled form state with field-level validation
Static syntax highlighting at build time (Page 12)
HTTP + WS server — replaces Express for lower overhead
WS upgrade handling; subscribes Redis pub/sub
Per-IP rate limits on auth + safety endpoints
httpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie for refresh token
Type-safe DB queries; transaction support for registration
Redis client — pub/sub, pipeline, TTL management
RS256 JWT sign + verify — private key never leaves server
Transactional email — SOS, late night alerts
GPS simulator + ML microservice — async ASGI
Model serialisation — loads eta_model.joblib at startup
Redis client for ML feature assembly from bus state
Primary persistence — users, fleet, safety, routes
Real-time state, pub/sub, session store, ETA cache
Backend service hosting — no Docker constraint
IZUM | System Architecture & Build Phases | ThinkRoot × Vortex Hackathon 2026
